truth_sources_allowed: server-owned metadata only if already configured
forbidden_first_pass: claiming setup readiness without command truth
admission_notes: Static progress/navigation can be rendered, but setup truth must come from strict JSON packets.
Manual path controls are not admitted in web UI because browser-origin local paths are forbidden.
Automatic discovery needs a dedicated command-owned discovery surface before implementation.
desktop_transfer_notes: Manual path selection may be reconsidered only in desktop/native picker phase after owner approval.
Desktop renderer still must pass candidate ids or command-owned references, not raw shell strings.

future_actions_allowed_only_after_separa: save selected candidate through strict JSON command
support open-location action for server-owned known location
forbidden_first_pass: treat launch client as path verification
claim selected profile success without command truth
admission_notes: Current web command adapter contains launch_client as disabled-by-default and only server-supplies launch_client_path.
Launch client is dispatch truth only and must not be repurposed as a selector or verifier.
A browser-safe implementation needs server-owned discovery result ids.
desktop_transfer_notes: Desktop may later own native picker UX, but command semantics must remain strict JSON and bounded.
Selection persistence needs a command API contract before visual controls become active.

truth_sources_allowed: legacy import --source-dir <path> --json only after safe server-owned source-dir mediation
future_actions_allowed_only_after_separa: create snapshot through explicit command-owned surface
verify import plan through non-mutating strict JSON dry-run
apply import through server-owned source-dir mediation and strong confirmation
rollback through explicit command-owned rollback surface
forbidden_first_pass: direct scan of legacy config/state/auth files from UI
dynamic account-count claims without command packet
import apply without strong confirmation and post-action refresh
rollback button without command-owned rollback contract
admission_notes: The CLI command exists, but source-dir path handling is not a safe web-browser payload.
The render's account counts and safety statuses are dynamic truth claims and require strict JSON import-plan evidence.
Legacy import implementation remains separate from first-pass settings and setup display.
desktop_transfer_notes: Desktop import UX should start from discovery/dry-run evidence, not arbitrary path submission.
Snapshot and rollback affordances require explicit command contracts before activation.
